description: Calculate the sinuosity index of a Mississippi River section between 'Sinuosity_Start' (33.4000°N, 91.0500°W) and 'Sinuosity_End' (33.2500°N, 91.2000°W). Measure both the straight-line distance and the actual river channel length. Create a 'Sinuosity_Analysis' placemark at the midpoint with the straight-line distance, channel length, sinuosity index (channel/straight, 2 decimal places), and classification ('Straight' <1.3, 'Sinuous' 1.3-1.5, 'Meandering' >1.5). Save all placemarks in a folder called 'Mississippi_Sinuosity' and export to ~/Documents/mississippi_sinuosity.kml.
